texts.full_text: taff satisfaction, positive patient outcomes and the cost effectiveness of staff retention. Two focus groups, one group of nine NUMs and one group of five staff nurses, were conducted at an Australia public hospital. A descriptive phenomenological approach informed data generation and data analysis. The NUMs reported that they were not adequately trained in the skills required to effectively manage staff conflict such as manipulation and bitterness, requiring disciplinary intervention on an ongoing basis. The consequences included reduced staff morale, decreased staff satisfaction, increased stress to the NUM and ultimately retention issues for both the NUM and Unit staff. This study highlights the potential impact of inadequate implementation and understanding of HRM policy and practice by NUMS on the front line. It’s a busy time full of big feelings (for everyone involved).… January 15, 2018 motherhood 18 Months A few weeks ago, my little boy turned 18 months old. It seems unreal to me that he’s been around that long. That I am a year and a half into the best and most challenging job* I’ve ever had.… March 9, 2017 motherhood Confessions of a part time solo parent I started writing this a few months ago, when my son was 10 months old and I was still grappling with the concept that I’m sort of raising him on my own for significant stretches. Being his primary caregiver while also navigating work commitments has been an ongoing challenge. It’s not always easy to balance everything—there are days where it feels like a juggling act gone wrong, and all you can do is laugh at the absurdity of trying to keep so many balls in the air when your toddler decides that today would be perfect for their first full-blown tantrum. But there's something incredibly rewarding about seeing him grow through these moments. I’ve learned more from this experience than I ever thought possible—patience, adaptability, and a deep sense of resilience are just some of what motherhood has taught me so far. It’s not always pretty or perfect; it can be messy and exhausting but also full of love and joy that makes every challenge worth facing. I’m still figuring things out one day at a time—just like many other solo parents—and I wouldn’t change this journey for the world, even if sometimes (or often) you just need to take five minutes in your car with some quiet music or coffee. Those moments are part of what keeps us going and remind me that it’s okay not always having all the answers. This value should point to the endpoint from which Kubernetes will retrieve signing certificates, typically known as JWKS or JSON Web Key Set. - Example: If you're using Google Cloud Platform (GCP) for OIDC authentication and have created a service account that acts like an identity provider, then your issuer URL might look something similar to this: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v4/certs ``` ### Refresh Token (refresh_token) The `client-secret` is not typically used with refresh tokens in OIDC. texts.full_text: decipher the etiopathogenesis of malformations and diseases. The Hedgehog and retinoic acid (RA) signaling pathways play roles during embryogenesis, organogenesis, and tissue homeostasis \[[@B1-ijms-20-02275],[@B2-ijms-20-02275],[@B3-ijms-20-02275],[@B4-ijms-20-02275],[@B5-ijms-20-02275],[@B6-ijms-20-02275],[@B7-ijms-20-02275],[@B8-ijms-20-02275],[@B9-ijms-20-02275],[@B10-ijms-20-02275],[@B11-ijms-20-02275],[@B12-ijms-20-02275],[@B13-ijms-20-02275]\], and genetic disruption of Hedgehog signaling can lead to \[[@B1-ijms-20-02275],[@B14-ijms-20-02275], [@B15-ijms-20-02275]\]. The Hedgehog (Hh) signaling pathway is essential for the regulation of cell proliferation, differentiation, and tissue patterning during development. It involves a series of proteins including Sonic hedgehog (Shh), Indian hedgehog (Ihh), Desert hedgehog (D hh ), Patched1 (Ptch1), Smoothened (Smo), Gli transcription factors (Gli1-3), among others \[[@B2-ijms-20-02275], [@B4-ijms-20-02275]\]. Activation of the Hh pathway typically occurs when Hedgehog ligands bind to their receptor, Patched. This binding releases inhibition on Smo and allows it to activate downstream signaling cascades that ultimately lead to changes in gene expression mediated by Gli transcription factors \[[@B3-ijms-20-02275], [@B16-ijms-20-02275]\]. On the other hand, retinoic acid (RA) signaling is critical for various processes including anterior–posterior patterning of embryos and differentiation in multiple tissues \[[@B8-ijms-20-02275], [@B13-ijms-20-02275]\]. RA acts through nuclear receptors, such as retinoic acid receptor (RAR) α/β/γ and the retinoid X receptor (RXR), which form heterodimers that bind to DNA sequences known as Retinoic Acid Response Elements (RAREs).

Many residents and visitors have pointed out that while the city boasts a rich cultural scene, there are certain aspects where it falls short compared to other major cities like New York or Los Angeles [2]. Chicago's weather can be another source of frustration for its inhabitants—the city is known for experiencing extreme cold winters and hot summers. The unpredictable nature of Chicago's climate has led many residents to develop a sense of resilience, but also an underlying dissatisfaction with the conditions they have no control over. Moreover, there are certain socio-economic factors that contribute to unhappiness among some Chicagoans [3]. While the city boasts a strong economy and numerous job opportunities in sectors such as finance, technology, and healthcare, it still struggles with issues like income inequality. The stark disparity between different neighborhoods can create feelings of division and resentment. texts.full_text: State Route 22is an important east-west highway running through central Virginia. It starts at US Highway 58 in Mecklenburg County near the North Carolina border and ends on U.S. Route 29 (US-29) just outside of Richmond, the state capital. Route 22 is approximately 200 miles long and traverses several significant towns including Lynchburg and Danville before heading west towards Lexington where it intersects with Interstate 81 at Exit 195B for a short distance to provide access from I-66. The route then continues eastward through Rockbridge County, crossing the Blue Ridge Mountains via Route 250 (the Shenandoah Valley Turnpike) and entering Amherst County. Throughout its journey across Virginia's central region, State Route 22 serves as an essential artery for both local commuters and long-distance travelers. texts.full_text: Carbon Dots: Synthesis and Properties. Carbon dots (CDs) are new materials with applications in bioimaging, optical devices, catalysis, and many other fields. Their advantages, such as ease of large-scale preparation, low-costing precursors, highly tunable photoluminescence, satisfactory biocompatibility, and photostability against photobleaching, make them competitive alternatives to conventional semiconductor-based quantum dots and organic dyes. To overwhelm other luminescent materials in applications, their functionalities stably and tunably should be increased. To date, in addition to their luminescence, there should be other functionalities, and their functionalities should be stably and tunably. texts.full_text: I went to Shangri-La directly from the office. Veve and Nyo dropped me at KarangMenjanganHospital and I took a taxi from there. I arrived in the hotel first. I marched to the toilet and fix my make up. Actually I didn’t wear any make up from the office, so it wasn’t really fixing. Rina came about half an hour later and off we went to Desperados, our favourite club. Arief, unsurprisingly, called Rina, just a moment before she left for the club, that he wasn’t feeling good. Yeah yeah.. As usual, she ordered a glass of coke and me some small Bintang beer. We sat in a dim corner, a strategic place to watch the band and spy on some couples,who were awkwardly trying to dance to the upbeat music. We were having a good time, laughing and joking, when a peculiar-looking man caught our attention. He was a gelandangan, a street urchin, and seemed to be out of place among the club's fashionable crowd. He was trying to sell something, but the bouncers were having none of it, and were trying to usher him out. Just as the man was about to be thrown out, a well-dressed executive, who seemed to be in his late 30s, appeared out of nowhere and started talking to the gelandangan. texts.full_text: increasing ZO‐1 expression in mice.[4](#cns13101-bib-0004){ref-type="ref"} The unanimous change of ZO‐1 and BBB integrity strongly implied the key role of ZO‐1 in maintaining normal BBB structure and function. Beside ZO‐1 expression, the BBB integrity is also affected by the phosphorylation status of ZO‐1, because phosphorylated ZO‐1 may lose its normal function. It was reported that TNF‐α could reduce ZO‐1 expression, while enhance ZO‐1 phosphorylation in human brain microvascular endothelial cells, thus potentially affecting BBB permeability.[10](#cns13101-bib-0010){ref-type="ref"} Animal study also indicated that in dystrophic mice brains, decreased ZO‐1 expression, and increased Zo‐1 phosphorylation were correlated with impaired BBB integrity.[31](#cns13101-bib-0031){ref-type="ref"} In the present study, we reported that hypoxia may reduce ZO‐1 expression while increase ZO‐1 phosphorylation, leading to BBB integrity impairment. Numerous studies indicated that the neuroprotective effect of propofol against BBB impairment could be mediated through multiple pathways. We found in an ongoing in vitro study that propofol could modulate TNF‐α‐induced matrix metalloprotein‐9 (MMP‐9) expression and activation, thus alleviating TNF‐α‐induced extracellular matrix breakdown and basal membrane damage (data not shown). Consistently, propofol was found to protect the BBB by decreasing MMP‐9 expression and improve the neurobehavioral outcome in rats.[26](#cns13101-bib-0026){ref-type="ref"} Others also reported that propofol could reverse ischemia‐downregulated expression of claudins and occludins, thus exerting protective effect on BBB in rabbits.[16](#cns13101-bib-0016){ref-type="ref"} In this study, we demonstrated that the neuroprotective effect of propofol against hypoxia‐impaired BBB was correlated with increased ZO‐1 expression and decreased ZO‐1 phosphorylation. texts.full_text: iabetes is characterized by a near-absolute deficiency of insulin secretion. More recently, advances in genetics and a better appreciation of the atypical features of DM has resulted in more categories of diabetes. In the context of kidney disease, patients with DM and microalbuminuria are more inline to have classical type 2 or monogenic forms while those who develop macroproteinuric renal failure resemble individuals from ethnic minorities presenting as young adults (youth-onset) without insulin resistance. ## Introduction The term 'diabetes' was first coined by the Greek physician Aretaeus of Cappadocia in his book _On Diseases_ around 100 ad. The word diabetes means a siphon or to run through, and this refers specificallyto polyuria [1]. It is only recently that we have begun appreciating how heterogeneous DM actually can be clinically. DM has been traditionally classified into type-2 (T\({}_{z}\)D), which comprises 90% of all individuals with diabetes; insulin-dependent or ketosis-prone TID, also known as juvenile-onset and adult onset. The latter two terms are imprecise because young people may have classical non-insulin dependent DM [NIDD] while adults can develop IDDM. More recently the American Diabetes Association (ADA) has proposed a new classification that is based upon pathophysiology rather than age of presentation: insulin resistance, impaired \(\beta\)-cell function and/or excessive hepatic glucose production. This definition does not account for an increasingly recognized group with diabetes secondary to genetic mutations [2]. DM may be classified into the following categories: * Type-1 Monogenic forms (maturity-onset DM in youth) Type 3a: gestational diabetics who progress and remain diabetic postpartum. ### Insulin resistance syndrome, metabolic X or Syndrome Z - a form of type-z diabetes? In this chapter we shall focus on the insulin resistant syndromes that are increasingly recognized as being associated with renal disease. We will also discuss briefly monogenic forms because these patients may develop microalbuminuria and should be differentiated from classical T\({}_{z}\)D. ### Insulin resistance syndrome or metabolic X Insuin is

texts.full_text: Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, Ukraine, United Arab Emirates, the UK, and the USA. The report includes a detailed analysis of key market dynamics such as drivers, restraints, trends, opportunities, supply chain structure, competitive landscape, pricing strategies, import/export scenarios, production patterns, consumption levels, technological advancements in extraction methods, environmental regulations impacting nickel mining activities globally. Additionally, it highlights significant mergers and acquisitions (M&A), partnerships, expansions by major players operating within the unwrought nickel market. The study also segments the global unwrought nickel industry based on product type into primary refined nickel and secondary or recycled nickel; application-wise segmentation covers stainless steel production, electroplating processes, batteries for electric vehicles & consumer electronics devices, alloy manufacturing among others. By providing insights through quantitative data supported by qualitative analysis, this report serves as an essential resource for stakeholders involved in strategic planning related to investments within the unwrought nickel sector across regions worldwide. texts.full_text: purpose or benefit under federal law, and as an alien present in the United States without being admitted or paroled. On June 8, 2009, Ye Gon was convicted of conspiracy to distribute ephedrine knowing it would be used illegally to manufacture methamphetamine (in violation of 21 U.S.C. § 846) and sentenced on December 7, 2009 to a term of imprisonment followed by supervised release. ### Proceedings Before the Immigration Judge On January 5, 2010, an IJ conducted a hearing where Ye Gon conceded removability but sought relief from removal under various grounds. The IJ denied his applications for asylum, withholding of removal, and protection under the Convention Against Torture (CAT), finding that he was statutorily ineligible due to his conviction. ### Appeal Before the BIA Ye Gon appealed the decision to the Board of Immigration Appeals (BIA). On December 17, 2010, the BIA affirmed in part with modifications. The BIA agreed that Ye Gon's convictions rendered him categorically statutorily ineligible for asylum and withholding of removal but remanded on other issues. ### Petition for Review On March 30, 2011, Ye Gon filed a petition for review in this Court challenging the final order of removal. He argues primarily that: - The IJ erred by failing to consider his claims under CAT and withholding from Mexico. - His due process rights were violated during proceedings before both the IJ and BIA. ### Legal Standards #### Standard of Review The court reviews decisions of the BIA for substantial evidence, treating factual findings as conclusive unless any reasonable adjudicator would be compelled to conclude otherwise. See 8 U.S.C. § 1252(b)(4). The Court also conducts de novo review of questions of law and mixed questions of fact and law. #### Relief from Removal To qualify for withholding of removal, an applicant must show a clear probability that his life or freedom would be threatened in the proposed country on account of race,

His strategy was built on patience, discipline, and investing in companies with strong fundamentals that could grow over time. ### The Long-Term Perspective The long-term approach is often referred to as the "Dragon Scroll" of investing because it seems simple but requires a rare level of commitment and understanding. Here are some key principles: 1. Focus on Quality: Invest in businesses you understand, with strong competitive advantages (moats), solid financials, and competent management. 2.
